cards.title: US Government Bans Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5
cards.body: The US government forced Anthropic to pull its two newest models citing national security concerns. Amazon researchers allegedly found a way to bypass Fable 5's guardrails. Cybersecurity experts are now evaluating the implications.

cards.title: Export Controls on AI: History Suggests Limited Effectiveness
cards.body: For 30 years, cybersecurity software export controls have proven ineffective. The same pattern may repeat with Anthropic's Mythos cybersecurity model. Questions remain about whether restrictions can actually stop technology flows.

cards.title: Anthropic Ban May Accidentally Boost Brand Recognition
cards.body: The US government's forced pullback of Fable 5 and Mythos 5 is generating significant media attention. Market response suggests the ban may paradoxically increase brand awareness. Anthropic's numbers reportedly remain unaffected by the regulatory action.

cards.title: Reliance Weaving AI Into 500M+ User Services
cards.body: Billionaire Ambani's Reliance is integrating AI across telecom services, apps, and home devices. The initiative reaches over 500 million users across India. This represents a massive scale-up of AI consumer deployment.

cards.title: Allbirds CEO Launches AI Startup With Solo Team
cards.body: The new venture operates with a single founder and substantial seed funding. Path forward and team-building plans remain unclear. The startup aims to apply AI to commerce and sustainability.

cards.title: US Questions ASML Chip Tool Availability in China
cards.body: The US government alleges ASML's advanced chip tools may have reached Chinese customers. Commercial incentives suggest ASML would avoid jeopardizing its export license. Details about how this occurred remain disputed.

cards.title: Elastic Acquires Deductive AI for Up to $85M
cards.body: Deductive AI, founded three years ago, specializes in using AI to catch and resolve software bugs. The acquisition supports Elastic's expansion into AI-driven development tools. Deal structure includes performance-based earnout provisions.

cards.title: AI Inference Startup Baseten Raising $1.5B Round
cards.body: Baseten is finalizing a $1.5 billion funding round at a $13 billion valuation. The move comes months after the company's previous mega-round. The raise reflects growing investor appetite for AI inference infrastructure.

cards.title: Snap Spins Off AI Video Team Into Dotmo
cards.body: Snapchat is creating Dotmo, a new company focused on AI video development. The spinoff includes current Snap staff transitioning to the new venture. Cost pressures prompted the structural separation.

cards.title: OpenAI Recruits Top Talent Ahead of IPO
cards.body: OpenAI hired Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball. The moves strengthen leadership before the company's public offering. Both additions signal focus on research and policy domains.

cards.title: 47% of US Singles Wary of AI in Dating
cards.body: Nearly half of American singles view AI negatively in dating contexts, per Match data. However, many users accept AI for profile optimization and conversation suggestions. Sentiment varies significantly by application and transparency.

cards.title: Amazon Challenges Nvidia by Selling AI Chips
cards.body: AWS is negotiating deals to sell its AI chips to other data centers. CEO Andy Jassy estimates this represents a $50 billion opportunity. The move positions Amazon as a direct competitor to Nvidia's dominance.

cards.title: FERC Grants Data Centers Fast-Track Grid Access
cards.body: Federal regulators mandated fast lanes for AI data center grid interconnections. The order accelerates infrastructure access but doesn't address electricity supply constraints. Implementation could reshape energy market dynamics.

cards.title: Slow Tech Movement Challenges AI-Driven Attention Crisis
cards.body: Consumers increasingly seek technology that respects attention and autonomy. The slow tech trend offers alternatives to algorithmically-driven experiences. Users prioritize control over convenience and personalization.

cards.title: Disabling Google's Gemini AI in Docs Made Easy
cards.body: Google Docs users can disable AI-generated suggestions and "write with Gemini" prompts. The process requires simple settings adjustments. Many users find these notifications intrusive.

cards.title: Karamo Brown Launches AI-Powered Wellness App Kē
cards.body: The "Queer Eye" host created Kē featuring an AI digital clone for wellness guidance. Brown spent 18 months developing content covering fitness, nutrition, and personal growth. The app aims to democratize access to his coaching expertise.

cards.title: General Intuition Seeks $300M at $2B Valuation
cards.body: The startup develops embodied AI and world models using Medal's 2 billion video dataset. General Intuition represents the growing intersection of robotics and generative AI. Funding round reflects confidence in embodied AI applications.

cards.title: Tech Worker PAC Challenges Big Tech With $5M Budget
cards.body: Guardrails PAC positions itself as a populist counterweight to Big Tech's political spending. The organization runs on small donations from AI industry workers. It raises $5M against Big Tech's $100M+ influence.

cards.title: Pixi Transforms Text Messages Into AR Experiences
cards.body: Pixi's new iOS app converts text messages into interactive augmented reality. The platform replaces traditional stickers and emoji with immersive AR reactions. This evolution signals next-generation mobile messaging paradigms.

cards.title: Sequoia Leader Roelof Botha Joins SpaceX Board
cards.body: Former Sequoia Capital leader fills board vacancy at newly public SpaceX. Botha's appointment comes after SpaceX completed the largest IPO ever. His venture expertise strengthens the aerospace company's leadership.

cards.title: US Government Bans Anthropic's Latest AI Models
cards.body: The US government forced Anthropic to pull its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models citing national security concerns after Amazon researchers found a way to bypass Fable 5's guardrails. Cybersecurity experts are questioning whether export controls will actually stop AI model proliferation.

cards.title: Reliance Weaves AI Into Telecom for 500M Users
cards.body: Billionaire Ambani's Reliance is integrating AI across telecom services, apps, and smart homes for over 500 million people. The move represents a massive-scale deployment of AI technology in everyday consumer services.

cards.title: Elastic Acquires Bug-Catching AI Startup Deductive
cards.body: Elastic is buying Deductive AI for up to $85 million, a startup that uses AI to identify and resolve software bugs. The acquisition highlights growing demand for AI-powered developer tools.

cards.title: AI Inference Startup Baseten Raises $1.5 Billion
cards.body: Baseten is closing a $1.5 billion funding round at a $13 billion valuation as AI inference becomes the hottest sector. The mega-round comes just months after the startup's previous massive funding.

cards.title: Snap Spins Off AI Video Team Into Dotmo
cards.body: Snapchat is spinning off its internal AI video development team into a new company called Dotmo to focus on AI without the overhead costs. The move signals Snap's commitment to AI video technology.

cards.title: OpenAI Hires Top AI Talent Ahead of IPO
cards.body: OpenAI is recruiting Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball. The hiring spree suggests OpenAI is bulking up before its anticipated IPO.

cards.title: Amazon Challenges Nvidia by Selling AI Chips
cards.body: AWS is in talks to sell its custom AI chips to other data centers, representing a potential $50 billion opportunity. Amazon aims to compete directly with Nvidia's dominance in AI hardware.

cards.title: Startup Claims Breakthrough in LLM Bottleneck
cards.body: Miami-based Subquadratic emerged from stealth claiming it solved a decade-old mathematical bottleneck limiting large language model performance. The announcement came with thin details but has attracted significant investor interest.

cards.title: General Intuition Raises $300M for Embodied AI
cards.body: General Intuition is in talks to raise $300 million at a $2 billion valuation for developing embodied AI and world models. The startup leverages Medal's massive dataset of 2 billion videos annually.

cards.title: Karamo Brown Launches AI Wellness App Clone
cards.body: The Queer Eye coach unveiled Kē, a wellness app featuring his AI digital clone to help users with fitness, meditation, and personal growth. Brown spent 18 months developing the platform based on his own wellness journey.
